y="90" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="aRLp-qEVFRu9UhOtxowT-2" parent="aRLp-qEVFRu9UhOtxowT-1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#000075;fontColor=#FFFFFF;fontStyle=1;fontSize=19;" value="<span style="font-size: 50px;">PACIO ToC Use Case: IG Example Data</span>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="60" width="1470" x="10" y="10" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="aRLp-qEVFRu9UhOtxowT-4" parent="aRLp-qEVFRu9UhOtxowT-1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#D9D9D9;fontColor=#333333;strokeColor=none;fontSize=18;verticalAlign=top;align=left;fontFamily=Verdana;spacingLeft=4;spacingRight=4;spacingBottom=3;spacingTop=3;" value="<p style="margin: 0in; caret-color: rgb(33, 33, 33); color: rgb(33, 33, 33);" class="MsoNormal"><font color="#000000" face="Aptos, sans-serif"><span style="font-size: 19px; caret-color: rgb(33, 33, 33);">Betsy Smith-Johnson is a 66-year-old Medicare-eligible woman admitted to Happy Nursing Facility in Grand Rapids, Michigan, following a cerebral infarction that resulted in left-sided hemiparesis. Her care, overseen by Dr. Gerald Park, addressed both her recovery and pre-existing chronic conditions through skilled nursing and rehabilitative therapy. Upon achieving sufficient functional recovery, the SNF care team determined that discharge to home with home health support was appropriate.</span></font></p><p style="margin: 0in; caret-color: rgb(33, 33, 33); color: rgb(33, 33, 33);" class="MsoNormal"><font color="#000000" face="Aptos, sans-serif"><span style="font-size: 19px; caret-color: rgb(33, 33, 33);"><br></span></font></p><p style="margin: 0in; caret-color: rgb(33, 33, 33); color: rgb(33, 33, 33);" class="MsoNormal"><font color="#000000" face="Aptos, sans-serif"><span style="font-size: 19px; caret-color: rgb(33, 33, 33);">In preparation for discharge, Happy Nursing Facility assembled a transitions of care (ToC) bundle containing Betsy's active problem list, allergy record, reconciled medication list, and immunization history — each expressed as discrete, coded data elements that are both machine- and human-readable. This bundle was transmitted electronically to Motown Home Health Grand Rapids ahead of discharge, enabling the receiving team to begin care planning before Betsy arrived home and eliminating the need to reconstruct her clinical history from unstructured fax and phone-based sources.</span></font></p><p style="margin: 0in; caret-color: rgb(33, 33, 33); color: rgb(33, 33, 33);" class="MsoNormal"><font color="#000000" face="Aptos, sans-serif"><span style="font-size: 19px; caret-color: rgb(33, 33, 33);"><br></span></font></p><p style="margin: 0in; caret-color: rgb(33, 33, 33); color: rgb(33, 33, 33);" class="MsoNormal"><font color="#000000" face="Aptos, sans-serif"><span style="font-size: 19px; caret-color: rgb(33, 33, 33);">Because the structured data was directly consumable by the receiving EHR, the home health team was able to conduct an initial visit on the day of discharge, complete the OASIS admission assessment without manual re-entry, and have Betsy's critical ACE inhibitor allergy surfaced automatically before any new medication orders were placed. Dr. Anita Chu, the receiving physician, reviewed the ToC bundle and co-signed the home health plan of care, with occupational and physical therapy referrals already in place to support Betsy's continued recovery at home.</span></font></p>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="610" width="640" x="10" y="130" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="aRLp-qEVFRu9UhOtxowT-3" parent="aRLp-qEVFRu9UhOtxowT-1" style=""><font style="font-size: 24px;">Background &amp; Scene 1</font></b>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="50" width="650" x="10" y="80" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 1468px; height: 1px; padding-top: 30px; margin-left: 1px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #FFFFFF;
display: inline-block; font-size: 19px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #FFFFFF; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.span: PACIO ToC Use Case: IG Example Data

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 630px; height: 1px; padding-top: 130px; margin-left: 6px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #333333;
display: inline-block; font-size: 18px; font-family: Verdana; color: #333333; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

font.span: Betsy Smith-Johnson is a 66-year-old Medicare-eligible woman admitted to Happy Nursing Facility in Grand Rapids, Michigan, following a cerebral infarction that resulted in left-sided hemiparesis. Her care, overseen by Dr. Gerald Park, addressed both her recovery and pre-existing chronic conditions through skilled nursing and rehabilitative therapy. Upon achieving sufficient functional recovery, the SNF care team determined that discharge to home with home health support was appropriate.

In preparation for discharge, Happy Nursing Facility assembled a transitions of care (ToC) bundle containing Betsy's active problem list, allergy record, reconciled medication list, and immunization history — each expressed as discrete, coded data elements that are both machine- and human-readable. This bundle was transmitted electronically to Motown Home Health Grand Rapids ahead of discharge, enabling the receiving team to begin care planning before Betsy arrived home and eliminating the need to reconstruct her clinical history from unstructured fax and phone-based sources.

Because the structured data was directly consumable by the receiving EHR, the home health team was able to conduct an initial visit on the day of discharge, complete the OASIS admission assessment without manual re-entry, and have Betsy's critical ACE inhibitor allergy surfaced automatically before any new medication orders were placed. Dr. Anita Chu, the receiving physician, reviewed the ToC bundle and co-signed the home health plan of care, with occupational and physical therapy referrals already in place to support Betsy's continued recovery at home.
